user_intent_preservation.original_goal: Implement 3-phase PWM motor control on KIT_A2G_TC387_5V_TFT using TOM1 channels 1..6
user_intent_preservation.functional_behavior: Generate 20 kHz center-aligned PWM on TOM1 CH1..CH6 mapped to P00.2..P00.7 with synchronous multi-channel updates every 500 ms; initialize U=25%, V=50%, W=75% and step all three phases by +10% wrapping 0..100%. Keep IfxGtm_Tom_Timer as the time base.
user_intent_preservation.technical_approach: Migrate from IfxGtm_Tom_PwmHl to the unified high-level IfxGtm_Pwm for synchronized multi-channel PWM on GTM TOM1, driven by an IfxGtm_Tom_Timer time base. Preserve existing pin macros/channel pairing, configure center-aligned mode and 20 kHz using the PWM driver and TOM timer, and update duties using the array-based immediate multi-channel update API for atomic synchronous updates.

driver_selection.selection_rationale: Unified PWM driver supports multi-channel synchronized PWM, complementary pairs, center-aligned mode, and clean migration path from TOM PwmHl while retaining TOM1 as the submodule. It is explicitly recommended and available in the template.

runtime_function.name: GTM_TOM_3_Phase_Inverter_PWM_stepDuty
runtime_function.purpose: Every 500 ms, increment U/V/W duty by +10% with wrap to 0% if >100%, then atomically update all six TOM1 channels using the multi-channel immediate update API for synchronous output change.

driver_changes.reason: Unified high-level PWM with multi-channel sync; aligns with template recommendation

migration_notes.context_preserved: The PWM remains on GTM.TOM1 with channels 1..6 and the IfxGtm_Tom_Timer time base. Frequency (20 kHz), center-alignment, synchronous multi-channel updates, initial duties (U=25%, V=50%, W=75%), and stepping by +10% every 500 ms with wrap are all implemented using the unified IfxGtm_Pwm APIs and timer companion driver, while retaining existing pin macros and channel assignments. Note: retain the original pin macros for P00.2..P00.7; however, TC38x LFBGA516 pin mapping documentation indicates potential mismatches (e.g., P00.2 often maps to TOM1_5_TOUT11). Verify board routing and adjust only the pin macros if needed; the migration does not change them by design.
